subtasks.description:
Create src/display/Rectangle.mag — a Rectangle geometry class in the Alto namespace.

File must start with:
  namespace: 'Alto'
  import: 'Go'

Define: Rectangle subclass: Object
  instanceVars: x y width height

Class constructor:
  classMethod: x: anX y: aY width: aW height: aH [
    | r |
    r := self new.
    r x: anX.
    r y: aY.
    r width: aW.
    r height: aH.
    ^ r
  ]

Instance methods (use Maggie/Smalltalk syntax as seen in src/Main.mag):
  - x, y, width, height — getters (return instance var)
  - x:, y:, width:, height: — setters
  - containsPoint: aPoint — returns true if point (a 2-element array or object with x/y) is inside the rect. Check: px >= x and: [px < (x + width)] and: [py >= y] and: [py < (y + height)]. For simplicity, accept x and y as separate args: containsPointX: px y: py
  - intersect: other — return a new Rectangle that is the intersection of self and other. Compute maxX := (x max: other x). maxY := (y max: other y). minRight := ((x + width) min: (other x + other width)). minBottom := ((y + height) min: (other y + other height)). If minRight <= maxX or minBottom <= maxY, return nil (no intersection). Otherwise return Rectangle x: maxX y: maxY width: minRight - maxX height: minBottom - maxY.
  - extent — return a 2-element Array {width. height} or just provide right/bottom accessors
  - right — returns x + width
  - bottom — returns y + height

Refer to src/Main.mag for Maggie syntax conventions: method definitions use square brackets, string literals use single quotes, instance variable access is direct, dot terminates statements. The namespace is 'Alto' (single quotes). Comments use double quotes.

No dependencies on other subtasks — can run in parallel.

Create src/display/CellBuffer.mag — a 2D cell buffer for drawing operations.

File must start with:
  namespace: 'Alto'
  import: 'Go'

Define: CellBuffer subclass: Object
  instanceVars: cells width height

'cells' is a flat Array of size width*height. Each element is a Dictionary with keys: #char (integer charcode), #fg, #bg, #attrs. Index formula: y * width + x (use 1-based indexing since Maggie arrays are 1-based, so: y * width + x + 1).

Class constructor:
  classMethod: width: w height: h [
    | buf |
    buf := self new.
    buf initWidth: w height: h.
    ^ buf
  ]

Instance methods:
  - initWidth: w height: h — set width := w. height := h. Allocate cells array of size w*h, fill each with a default cell dict: Dictionary new at: #char put: 32; at: #fg put: nil; at: #bg put: nil; at: #attrs put: nil. (32 = space charcode)
  - width — return width
  - height — return height
  - indexX: x y: y — private helper, returns (y * width + x + 1) for 1-based array access. Should bounds-check: x >= 0, x < width, y >= 0, y < height.
  - cellAt: x y: y — return (cells at: (self indexX: x y: y))
  - putChar: ch x: x y: y style: style — bounds check, then: | cell idx | idx := self indexX: x y: y. cell := cells at: idx. cell at: #char put: ch. style is a Dictionary with optional #fg #bg #attrs keys; copy them into cell.
  - putString: str x: x y: y style: style — iterate characters of str. For each character at index i, call self putChar: (str at: i+1) charCode x: x+i y: y style: style. (Adjust for Maggie's string iteration — strings may support 'do:' or indexed access.)
  - drawBox: rect style: style — draw a box using Unicode box-drawing characters around the given Rectangle. Use charcodes: 9484 (┌ top-left), 9488 (┐ top-right), 9492 (└ bottom-left), 9496 (┘ bottom-right), 9472 (─ horizontal), 9474 (│ vertical). Draw top edge: putChar 9484 at rect x, rect y; then 9472 for rect x+1 to rect x+rect width-2; then 9488 at rect x+rect width-1, rect y. Draw bottom edge similarly with 9492/9496. Draw left/right sides with 9474 for y from rect y+1 to rect y+rect height-2.
  - clear — reset all cells to default (space, nil fg/bg/attrs). Iterate 1 to cells size, replace each with fresh default dict.
  - clearRect: rect — clear only cells within the given Rectangle bounds.

IMPORTANT Maggie syntax notes (from src/Main.mag):
  - Statements end with dot (.)
  - Method bodies in square brackets [...]
  - Local vars declared with | var1 var2 |
  - Use := for assignment
  - Array indexing is 1-based: (anArray at: index)
  - String methods: check if 'size' or 'length' exists; characters accessed via 'at:'
  - Keyword messages: receiver msg: arg1 next: arg2
  - Cascades use semicolon (;)

This subtask depends on Rectangle.mag existing (for drawBox: rect parameter and clearRect: rect parameter), but Rectangle is a simple value object — CellBuffer only reads rect's x, y, width, height methods. If needed, describe the expected Rectangle protocol in a comment. Can run in parallel with Rectangle implementation.

Create src/display/Display.mag — the global compositor and event loop.

File must start with:
  namespace: 'Alto'
  import: 'Go'

Define: Display subclass: Object
  instanceVars: screen buffer windows running

'screen' is a Go::Terminal Screen (from wrap/terminal/stubs.mag). 'buffer' is a CellBuffer sized to the screen. 'windows' is an ordered Array (back-to-front z-order). 'running' is a boolean for the event loop.

Class method:
  classMethod: new [
    | d screenSize w h |
    d := super new.
    d initDisplay.
    ^ d
  ]

Instance methods:
  - initDisplay — create screen: screen := Go::Terminal newScreen. screen init. screen enableMouse. screen clear. Get size: screenSize := screen size. (screen size returns width/height — check how to destructure; may return an Array or a Point). Set w/h from screenSize. buffer := CellBuffer width: w height: h. windows := Array new. running := false.

  - addWindow: w — windows add: w. (Adds to end = front of z-order.)

  - removeWindow: w — windows remove: w.

  - bringToFront: w — windows remove: w. windows add: w. (Move to end = front.)

  - render — Composite all windows onto the screen buffer, then flush to terminal.
    1. buffer clear.
    2. Iterate windows from first (back) to last (front):
       windows do: [:win |
         | winBuf winRect |
         winBuf := win buffer.    "Each window must have a 'buffer' method returning its CellBuffer"
         winRect := win frame.    "Each window must have a 'frame' method returning a Rectangle"
         "Blit winBuf onto buffer at winRect origin"
         0 to: winBuf height - 1 do: [:row |
           0 to: winBuf width - 1 do: [:col |
             | srcCell destX destY |
             destX := winRect x + col.
             destY := winRect y + row.
             (destX >= 0 and: [destX < buffer width]) ifTrue: [
               (destY >= 0 and: [destY < buffer height]) ifTrue: [
                 srcCell := winBuf cellAt: col y: row.
                 buffer putChar: (srcCell at: #char) x: destX y: destY style: srcCell
               ]
             ]
           ]
         ]
       ].
    3. Write buffer to terminal screen:
       0 to: buffer height - 1 do: [:y |
         0 to: buffer width - 1 do: [:x |
           | cell style |
           cell := buffer cellAt: x y: y.
           style := Go::V2::Style new.
           "Apply fg/bg from cell if not nil"
           (cell at: #fg) ifNotNil: [:fg | style := style foreground: fg].
           (cell at: #bg) ifNotNil: [:bg | style := style background: bg].
           screen setContent: x _: y _: (cell at: #char) _: style
         ]
       ].
    4. screen show.

  - run — Main event loop.
    running := true.
    [running] whileTrue: [
      self render.
      | event |
      event := screen pollEvent.
      "Dispatch to frontmost window (last in array)"
      windows isEmpty ifFalse: [
        (windows last) handleEvent: event
      ]
    ].
    screen fini.

  - stop — running := false.

IMPORTANT: The Go::Terminal and Go::V2::Style APIs are defined in:
  - wrap/terminal/stubs.mag: Screen has methods init, enableMouse, clear, setContent: x _: y _: mainc _: style, show, pollEvent, fini, size
  - wrap/tcell/stubs.mag: Style has methods foreground: c, background: c, bold: on, etc.

Note on setContent: the signature is 'setContent: x _: y _: mainc _: style' — it uses _: for positional args after the first keyword. See src/Main.mag line 15 for usage example: screen setContent: 10 _: 5 _: 65 _: (Go::V2::Style new).

This subtask depends on CellBuffer.mag and Rectangle.mag existing. It also assumes windows have 'buffer', 'frame', and 'handleEvent:' methods (which will be implemented in later phases). For now, the Display should be structurally complete and compilable — the window protocol can be documented in comments.

Also update src/Main.mag to use the new Display class instead of directly manipulating the screen. Replace the current Main>>start method to create a Display, possibly add a test window or just demonstrate the compositor with a simple demo (draw a box on the buffer, render it).
